intro: Roettlera schefferi, also known as Bupleurum chinense, is a flowering plant species in the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its therapeutic properties, with potential health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, and support liver function. The bioactive constituents of Roettlera schefferi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Roettlera schefferi include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried extracts and capsules, which are commonly used in Chinese and Ayurvedic medicine.
